title: Gujarati Undhiyu (Simplified Winter Vegetable Dish)

ingredients.name: potato, peeled and cut into large cubes
raw banana (kachche kele), peeled and cut into thick coins

subs.name: same quantity of yam (suran) or sweet potato cut into large cubes

ingredients.name: green beans, cut into 3 cm pieces
visual_checks: valor (flat beans) is traditional; regular beans work well
ingredients.notes: valor (flat beans) is traditional; regular beans work well

subs.name: 100 g flat beans (valor papdi) if available at Indian grocery stores

ingredients.name: fresh fenugreek leaves (methi), finely chopped
visual_checks: dried kasuri methi can substitute; use 2 tbsp
ingredients.notes: dried kasuri methi can substitute; use 2 tbsp

subs.name: 2 tbsp kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves); skip the salting step
3 tbsp desiccated coconut soaked in 2 tbsp warm water for 10 minutes

steps.text: Make the muthias first: combine besan, chopped fenugreek leaves, a pinch of turmeric, a pinch of salt, 0.5 tsp oil and just enough water to form a firm dough. Shape into marble-sized balls. Heat 2 tbsp oil in a kadai and shallow-fry the muthias in batches over medium flame until they turn golden and firm, about 3–4 minutes per batch. Drain on paper and set aside.

Grind together coconut, coriander leaves, green chillies, garlic, ginger, sesame seeds, cumin and sugar with a splash of water in a mixie to a coarse, vibrant green paste. Do not over-blend — a little texture is ideal.

In the same kadai, heat remaining oil on medium. Add the ground masala paste and fry, stirring continuously, for 3–4 minutes until the raw garlic smell disappears and the oil just starts to separate at the edges.

Add potato, raw banana and brinjal to the kadai and gently turn to coat every piece in the masala. Sprinkle remaining turmeric and salt. Add 0.5 cup water, cover tightly and cook on low flame for 15 minutes, shaking the pan every 5 minutes instead of stirring to prevent the vegetables from breaking.
Add the peas and beans, then nestle the fried muthias over the top. Cover and cook for another 8–10 minutes until the potatoes are fully tender when pierced and the peas are bright green.

Drizzle lemon juice over the top, taste for salt and give the pan a gentle fold. Serve hot with puris or phulkas and fresh curd. Undhiyu tastes even better on the second day after the masala has time to permeate all the vegetables.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: raw-banana → same quantity of yam (suran) or sweet potato cut into large cubes; coconut → 3 tbsp desiccated coconut soaked in 2 tbsp warm water for 10 minutes; fenugreek-leaves → 2 tbsp kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves); skip the salting step; beans → 100 g flat beans (valor papdi) if available at Indian grocery stores
